source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure IAM users are members of access groups and IAM policies are assigned only to access groups

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure IAM users are members of access groups and IAM policies are assigned only to access groups
input_row.description: Simplify and secure the access management process by using access groups when you assign access to groups of users with identical access needs.
input_row.rationale: You can create an access group so that you can organize a set of users and service IDs into a single entity that makes it easy for you to assign access. You can assign a single policy to the group instead of assigning the same access multiple times for each individual user or service ID. Impact: Using access groups reduces the number of policies that must be created and managed in the account and simplifies the process of adding new users or service IDs or modifying user and service IDs access.
input_row.audit: Console: Audit user access policies: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access (IAM) . 3. Click Users and select a user by clicking on the username. 4. Click Access Policies . ***If the user has individual access policies and you wish to remove them, complete the following steps. 5. Locate the row containing the access policy you wish to remove. Click the actions icon corresponding to that row and click Remove. API:N/A
input_row.remediation: Assign an access policy to an Access Group: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access(IAM) . 3. Click Access Groups . 4. Click an Access Group name, then click the Access Policies tab 5. Click Assign access . Add members to an Access Group: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access(IAM) . 3. Click Access Groups . 4. Click an Access Group name. 5. Click Add Users . Delete an access policy for a user: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menubar, click Manage > Access(IAM) . 3. Click Users. 4. Click on a user name. 5. Click the Access Policies tab 6. Locate the row containing the access policy you wish to remove. Click the actions icon corresponding to that row and click Remove. Default Value: By default, only the Public Access Group is enabled in IBM Cloud accounts. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-groups#groups
input_row.impact: Using access groups reduces the number of policies that must be created and managed in the account and simplifies the process of adding new users or service IDs or modifying user and service IDs access.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control involves checking if IAM users are members of access groups and if IAM policies are assigned to access groups. This can be automated by querying IBM Cloud IAM configurations using APIs or IBM Cloud CLI. Since IAM is a standard IBM Cloud service, and access group membership and policy assignments can be programmatically verified, this aligns with typical automated audit approaches.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_iam_access_group_membership_and_policy_assignment_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud API & CLI:** Utilize the IBM Cloud REST APIs related to IAM Access Groups and IAM Policies. You can use the IBM Cloud CLI to enumerate users, access groups, and policies.
- **Commands to Use:**
  - `ibmcloud iam access-groups` to list all access groups and their members.
  - `ibmcloud iam access-policies` to retrieve policies and check if they are associated with users or access groups.
- **Resources to Enumerate:**
  - List all IAM users in the account.
  - List all access groups and their members.
  - List all IAM policies and check their assignments.
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:**
  - Verify that each IAM user is a member of at least one access group.
  - Ensure all IAM policies are assigned to access groups, not directly to individual users.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** All IAM users are members of access groups, and all IAM policies are associated with access groups.
  - **Fail:** Any IAM user is not part of an access group, or any IAM policy is directly assigned to a user.

This approach ensures compliance with the CIS benchmark by validating access strategies while leveraging automation to efficiently manage and secure access control.
